forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c680
+project: expect
+title: Spec-timeout teardown should actively cancel the in-flight ACP session (Finding 3)
+---
+## What
+Confirmed during ^z8rwhhj (wire drive_and_revalidate into the live tool): the spec-timeout stop in `drive_and_revalidate` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs`) bounds the drive with `tokio::time::timeout`, which on elapse DROPS the drive future. Dropping tears down the `AgentPool` workers but does NOT send `session/cancel` for the in-flight ACP session — `AgentPool`'s `Drop` is synchronous and cannot await an async cancel over the connection it is dropping. The pool's idle/ceiling cancel path (its liveness supervisor) is the only path that actively cancels today, and that path is not on the spec-timeout teardown.
+
+A reproducing test exists and was adjusted to pin the guaranteed behavior (prompt typed `ExpectError::Timeout`, no hang): `drive::tests::spec_timeout_over_an_acp_session_aborts_the_in_flight_drive`. It deliberately does NOT assert the cancel (it doesn't happen). An earlier version asserting the cancel FAILED, confirming the gap.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] On spec-timeout, the in-flight ACP session is actively cancelled (`session/cancel` sent to the agent), not only its pool workers aborted.
+- [ ] A real-pipeline test over a mock-ACP `StallingAgent` (mirror `spec_timeout_over_an_acp_session_aborts_the_in_flight_drive`) asserts the cancel is sent, in addition to the typed timeout.
+- [ ] No hang and no regression to the existing stop-condition tests.
+
+## Notes
+Likely needs the spec-timeout to *actively* cancel before/while dropping (e.g. drive_and_revalidate or AcpGoalDriver issuing the cancel on the timeout branch), rather than relying on `AgentPool` `Drop`. Touches the engine (`drive.rs`) and possibly `swissarmyhammer-validators` `AgentPool` teardown.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,22 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw51s34jcj84e0v03s43hv3v
+  text: |-
+    Implemented (left in `doing` for review).
+
+    Approach: reused AgentPool's existing session/cancel path rather than a parallel cancel. Mapping spec.timeout directly onto the pool's `turn_ceiling` was rejected because the ceiling returns `PoolError::TurnCeiling` (surfaces as `ExpectError::Pool`), which breaks the acceptance requirement that the typed `ExpectError::Timeout` still be returned, and because the spec budget also bounds the non-agent observe path the ceiling doesn't cover. Instead added an out-of-band cancel handle:
+
+    - swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs: new `AgentPool::new_cancellable(.., cancel: CancellationToken)`; `new` delegates with a fresh never-fired token (review pipeline + all existing callers unchanged). `worker_loop`/`run_turn_with_liveness` thread the token; the supervisor `tokio::select!` gains a `cancel.cancelled()` arm that calls the extracted `cancel_in_flight_session` helper (shared with `abandon_turn`) and returns new `PoolError::TurnCancelled`.
+    - swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs: `GoalDriver::cancel(&self)` default no-op; `AcpGoalDriver` holds a `CancellationToken`, fires it in `cancel()`, threads a clone via new internal `run_expect_over_agent_with_cancel` → `run_pipeline_in_connection` → `new_cancellable`. Public `run_expect_over_agent` is a thin wrapper passing a never-cancelled token. `drive_and_revalidate` timeout branch now calls `driver.cancel()`, drains the drive for `SPEC_TIMEOUT_CANCEL_DRAIN` (1s, returns early once the cancelled turn resolves) so the pool worker transmits `session/cancel` over the still-live connection, then returns `ExpectError::Timeout`.
+    - tokio-util added to both crates (already a workspace dep; needed an awaitable/latching cancel primitive for the select arm).
+
+    Test (TDD, RED confirmed → GREEN): replaced `spec_timeout_over_an_acp_session_aborts_the_in_flight_drive` with `spec_timeout_over_an_acp_session_cancels_the_in_flight_session` — over a real AcpGoalDriver + mock StallingAgent it asserts BOTH `ExpectError::Timeout` (with the budget) AND that the agent recorded a `session/cancel`. Extracted shared `await_recorded_cancel` helper, also adopted by the idle test.
+
+    Verification: targeted `-E 'test(drive) or test(stop) or test(timeout) or test(revalidate)'` 35 pass; full `swissarmyhammer-expect` 311 pass; `swissarmyhammer-validators` pool tests 28 pass; `cargo check --workspace` clean; `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-expect -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt` applied. Adversarial double-check: PASS.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-27T17:24:30.994358+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '8280'
 project: expect

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -33,4 +33,12 @@
 - [ ] No hang and no regression to the existing stop-condition tests.

 ## Notes
-Likely needs the spec-timeout to *actively* cancel before/while dropping (e.g. drive_and_revalidate or AcpGoalDriver issuing the cancel on the timeout branch), rather than relying on `AgentPool` `Drop`. Touches the engine (`drive.rs`) and possibly `swissarmyhammer-validators` `AgentPool` teardown.
\ No newline at end of file
+Likely needs the spec-timeout to *actively* cancel before/while dropping (e.g. drive_and_revalidate or AcpGoalDriver issuing the cancel on the timeout branch), rather than relying on `AgentPool` `Drop`. Touches the engine (`drive.rs`) and possibly `swissarmyhammer-validators` `AgentPool` teardown.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-27 12:25)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs:254` — Public function `run_expect_over_agent` lacks documentation. This is a top-level entry point for the expect pipeline and needs a doc comment explaining its purpose, the semantic of its parameters, and how it differs from `run_expect_over_agent_with_cancel`. Add a doc comment (e.g., `/// Run the expect pipeline over an ACP agent, bounded by idle/ceiling deadlines only (no external cancel handle).`) and explain when to use this vs. the cancellable variant.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:102` — Public function `new` is a constructor that delegates to `new_cancellable` but lacks documentation explaining the relationship between the two constructors and when each should be used. Add a doc comment documenting that this creates a pool with no external cancel handle (bounded only by idle/ceiling timeouts), and reference `new_cancellable` as the alternative when an external cancel token is needed.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:158` — Public function `new_cancellable` is a newly added constructor variant that lacks documentation. Callers need to understand what the `cancel` parameter does and why this variant exists. Add a doc comment explaining that this constructor accepts an external `CancellationToken` that allows out-of-band cancellation of in-flight turns (used by expect's spec timeout), and explain the `cancel` parameter's semantics.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:320` — Public function `AgentPool::new_cancellable()` lacks a doc comment. All public items require documentation. Add a doc comment explaining the function's purpose and when to use it versus `new()`. Example: `/// Create a pool with an external cancellation handle that allows the caller to actively cancel in-flight sessions when a timeout budget elapses. This prevents orphaning agents behind dropped futures.`.
\ No newline at end of file

 ## Review Findings (2026-06-27 12:25)

 ### Warnings
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs:254` — Public function `run_expect_over_agent` lacks documentation. This is a top-level entry point for the expect pipeline and needs a doc comment explaining its purpose, the semantic of its parameters, and how it differs from `run_expect_over_agent_with_cancel`. Add a doc comment (e.g., `/// Run the expect pipeline over an ACP agent, bounded by idle/ceiling deadlines only (no external cancel handle).`) and explain when to use this vs. the cancellable variant.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:102` — Public function `new` is a constructor that delegates to `new_cancellable` but lacks documentation explaining the relationship between the two constructors and when each should be used. Add a doc comment documenting that this creates a pool with no external cancel handle (bounded only by idle/ceiling timeouts), and reference `new_cancellable` as the alternative when an external cancel token is needed.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:158` — Public function `new_cancellable` is a newly added constructor variant that lacks documentation. Callers need to understand what the `cancel` parameter does and why this variant exists. Add a doc comment explaining that this constructor accepts an external `CancellationToken` that allows out-of-band cancellation of in-flight turns (used by expect's spec timeout), and explain the `cancel` parameter's semantics.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:320` — Public function `AgentPool::new_cancellable()` lacks a doc comment. All public items require documentation. Add a doc comment explaining the function's purpose and when to use it versus `new()`. Example: `/// Create a pool with an external cancellation handle that allows the caller to actively cancel in-flight sessions when a timeout budget elapses. This prevents orphaning agents behind dropped futures.`.

   timestamp: 2026-06-27T17:24:30.994358+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw52b60bnrdcyt55x211x74x
+  text: |-
+    Cleared the 4 missing-doc review warnings. Doc comments are present and satisfy each finding:
+    - drive.rs run_expect_over_agent: documents purpose/params/errors plus inline notes on the run_expect_over_agent_with_cancel relationship (no external cancel handle; bounded by idle/ceiling only).
+    - pool.rs AgentPool::new: documents that it delegates to new_cancellable with a never-fired CancellationToken (cancellation disabled), references new_cancellable.
+    - pool.rs AgentPool::new_cancellable: documents the cancel param — firing it actively cancels the in-flight session (ACP session/cancel) resolving to PoolError::TurnCancelled, the seam the expect spec-timeout drives.
+

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -51,4 +51,11 @@
 - [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs:254` — Public function `run_expect_over_agent` lacks documentation. This is a top-level entry point for the expect pipeline and needs a doc comment explaining its purpose, the semantic of its parameters, and how it differs from `run_expect_over_agent_with_cancel`. Add a doc comment (e.g., `/// Run the expect pipeline over an ACP agent, bounded by idle/ceiling deadlines only (no external cancel handle).`) and explain when to use this vs. the cancellable variant.
 - [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:102` — Public function `new` is a constructor that delegates to `new_cancellable` but lacks documentation explaining the relationship between the two constructors and when each should be used. Add a doc comment documenting that this creates a pool with no external cancel handle (bounded only by idle/ceiling timeouts), and reference `new_cancellable` as the alternative when an external cancel token is needed.
 - [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:158` — Public function `new_cancellable` is a newly added constructor variant that lacks documentation. Callers need to understand what the `cancel` parameter does and why this variant exists. Add a doc comment explaining that this constructor accepts an external `CancellationToken` that allows out-of-band cancellation of in-flight turns (used by expect's spec timeout), and explain the `cancel` parameter's semantics.
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:320` — Public function `AgentPool::new_cancellable()` lacks a doc comment. All public items require documentation. Add a doc comment explaining the function's purpose and when to use it versus `new()`. Example: `/// Create a pool with an external cancellation handle that allows the caller to actively cancel in-flight sessions when a timeout budget elapses. This prevents orphaning agents behind dropped futures.`.
\ No newline at end of file
+- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:320` — Public function `AgentPool::new_cancellable()` lacks a doc comment. All public items require documentation. Add a doc comment explaining the function's purpose and when to use it versus `new()`. Example: `/// Create a pool with an external cancellation handle that allows the caller to actively cancel in-flight sessions when a timeout budget elapses. This prevents orphaning agents behind dropped futures.`.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-27 12:35)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs:34` — Protocol version `1` should be a named constant, not embedded as a magic number. This encodes the specific ACP protocol version and should be named for clarity and maintainability. Define `const ACP_PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = 1;` and use `InitializeRequest::new(ACP_PROTOCOL_VERSION.into())`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/drive.rs:476` — Test fixture value `40` assigned to 'total' field should be a named constant. This configures test behavior and its meaning is unexplained in context. Define `const TEST_TOTAL_VALUE: u32 = 40;` or provide a more descriptive constant name reflecting what this value represents in the test scenario.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/pool.rs:624` — Public method `PoolConfig::with_idle_timeout()` lacks a doc comment. Its sibling method `with_turn_ceiling()` immediately below is documented, creating an inconsistency. All public items require doc comments. Add a doc comment explaining the idle-timeout builder parameter, consistent with the pattern established by `with_turn_ceiling()`.
\ No newline at end of file
